newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. TDK's record FY3/2026 results, raised guidance, and AI-pivot capex plan (¥900B capex, 25-30% AI-DC CAGR target) were all disclosed at the April 28 FY briefing; the +2.8% move since June 9 is flow.

sources.label: TDK FY2026 slides: record profits, AI pivot (Investing.com)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha lens: no fundamental news since June 9; AI-DC capex/pivot was an April event. A +2.8% flow day pushes YTD to ~75% on a crowding-sensitive list. MLCC/inductor power-topology bottleneck intact but at ~$45B cap far less hidden than Tier A. Hold.
verdicts.bull: MLCC/inductor/film-cap content across the AI-DC power-delivery chain; ¥900B capex, 25-30% CAGR target.
verdicts.neutral: Core thesis solid but increasingly consensus; rerating consuming asymmetry.
verdicts.bear: +75% YTD, $45B cap, well-covered; limited residual alpha vs hidden peers.

verdicts.rankRationale: Broad board-level power/passives lens: no genuinely new info since June 9, only a +2.8% flow day. AI server PDN/MLCC/inductor positioning remains a credible bottleneck but the YTD rerating was already marked. Hold at 57.
verdicts.bull: Deep power-delivery content in AI servers; record earnings and rising capex.
verdicts.neutral: Quality compounder; rerating already reflects the AI tailwind.
verdicts.bear: Large, well-owned; cyclical consumer/components exposure remains.
